title: cerastium arvense subsp. commune
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. commune, commonly known as field chickweed, is a subspecies of annual or biennial herbaceous plant belonging to the pink family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been utilized to treat various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and digestive problems. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, providing numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Cerastium arvense subsp. commune can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of conditions.
